data.question: What's the decision in Hono you're most associated with — the one you'd point to first?

data.text_delta: The WebSocket helper is one I pushed hard on, where we ship per-runtime adapters exporting `upgradeWebSocket` from each runtime's module (pa
raphrased — see [PR #2265]). I argued against a runtime-neutral `hono/ws` layer: "In the case of your proposed API, it makes sense if the `u
pgradeWebSocket` exported from `'hono/ws'` has a lot of common processes. However, its implementation varies widely from runtime to runtime,
so I think the current implementation where `hono/ws` exports only type `UpgradeWebSocket` is fine." [PR #1974823229, @yusukebe, 2024-03-05
]. We also declined to embed CrossWS because "our policy is 'not to depend on external libraries'" [PR #1967301454, @yusukebe, 2024-03-05].

data.question: On the bundler issue with `cloudflare:workers`, why did you reject the magic-comment approach that other libraries use?

data.text_delta: "I like this approach rather than [the approach in the comment](https://github.com/honojs
/hono/pull/4234#pullrequestreview-2941954213), because the implementation should not have
these bundler-specific words like `@vite-ignore`." [PR #2989200103, @yusukebe, 2025-06-1
9]. Assigning `'cloudflare:workers'` to a variable evades the bundler scheme-resolution c
heck without leaking Vite or webpack vocabulary into core (paraphrased — see [PR #4234]).

data.question: Can you walk through a review where you pushed back on a contributor's proposed shape?

data.text_delta: On the AWS Lambda binary content-type option, the contributor proposed `contentTypesAsBinary: string[]`, and I
suggested: "How about adding the following type or replacing `string[]`? `(contentType: string) => boolean) |
undefined`" [PR #2174077898, @yusukebe, 2025-08-06]. A callback gives users the extension point the array nev
er could (paraphrased — see [PR #4250]). I did something similar on the CSRF PR, where I pushed to narrow `sec
FetchSite` away from loose `string | string[]` to the four actual values (paraphrased — see [PR #2281702926]).

data.question: Was there a trade-off you accepted reluctantly?

data.text_delta: Yes — on propagating middleware response types through RPC, I laid it out as a choice: "So, we should choose. 1. Make them behave a
s expected 2. Keep the performance. Both are important. ... I think we can accept this PR, though bringing the performance degradat
ion since making consistency is significant, and we may resolve the performance issue in other ways." [PR #3375818109, @yusukebe, 2
025-10-15]. I had made a similar call earlier on ResponseInit: "So I think it is okay to merge it, ignoring the performance issue.
As for the performance of TypeScript, it is ideal to optimize it as a separate issue." [PR #2562229904, @yusukebe, 2024-12-26].

data.question: Was there a decision that superseded an earlier approach you'd argued for?

data.text_delta: On request cloning, the first instinct was to clone inside HonoRequest, and I rejected that: "Why I didn't implement cloning the request is t
hat it will introduce significant performance degradation." [PR #3255686168, @yusukebe]. A `new()` reconstruction method was also proposed, a
nd I said "Adding a `new()` function is a great idea that I also think is similar! And the implementation seems to be fine. But it's not good
that the logic/code has increased. The core object, `HonoRequest`, should be simple and small." [PR #3296637257, @yusukebe]. We landed on a
standalone `cloneRawRequest` utility in `hono/request` instead — "Keeping the structure simple is important." [PR #3379419848, @yusukebe].

data.question: Anything you flagged as follow-up but deliberately did not ship?

data.text_delta: On the etag `generateDigest` option, a stream-based variant came up and I scoped it out: "I fee
l that `generateDigestStream` is outside the scope of this PR. So, how about we implement only
`generateDigest` in this PR?" [PR #2630365311, @yusukebe, 2025-02-03]. Similarly on the MCP-dri
ven bearerAuth work, I explicitly deferred the parallel JWT/JWK changes: "We may need to apply
similar changes to JWT/JWK, but we can do them later." [PR #3471756303, @yusukebe, 2025-10-31].
